title: End-to-end coralseed workflow: Lizard Island
text: tutorial runs core coralseed workflow simulated reseeding event Lizard Island: load reef benthic habitat maps; convert habitat classes spatial seascape settlement-probability surface; seed particles external dispersal model; simulate settlement; estimate settlement density settlement summaries; visualise output.
code: library(coralseed) library(tidyverse) library(sf) library(tmap) library(ggplot2)  sf::sf_use_s2(FALSE)

title: End-to-end coralseed workflow: Lizard Island
text: package includes Lizard Island example data inst/extdata. accessed system.file() tutorial works installation.
code: lizard_benthic_map <- system.file(   "extdata", "Lizard_Benthic.geojson",   package = "coralseed" ) |>   sf::st_read(quiet = TRUE)  lizard_reef_map <- system.file(   "extdata", "Lizard_Geomorphic.geojson",   package = "coralseed" ) |>   sf::st_read(quiet = TRUE)  lizard_particles_sf <- system.file(   "extdata", "lizard_del_14_1512_sim1_10.json",   package = "coralseed" ) |>   sf::st_read(quiet = TRUE)

title: End-to-end coralseed workflow: Lizard Island
text: seascape_probability() combines reef geomorphology benthic habitat maps assigns settlement probabilities habitat classes.
code: lizard_seascape <- seascape_probability(   reefoutline = lizard_reef_map,   habitat = lizard_benthic_map )  lizard_seascape Simple feature collection with 669 features and 3 fields Geometry type: POLYGON Dimension:     XY Bounding box:  xmin: 1628792 ymin: 8347643 xmax: 1633662 ymax: 8354525 Projected CRS: AGD84 / AMG zone 53 # A tibble: 669 × 4 # Groups:   class [7]    class                              geometry habitat_id settlement_probability  * <chr>                         <POLYGON [m]> <chr>                       <dbl>  1 Back Reef Slope ((1629141 8349637, 1629142… Back_Reef…                   0.46  2 Back Reef Slope ((1629009 8350083, 1629009… Back_Reef…                   0.54  3 Back Reef Slope ((1628986 8350109, 1628985… Back_Reef…                   0.57  4 Back Reef Slope ((1628986 8350114, 1628986… Back_Reef…                   0.45  5 Back Reef Slope ((1628996 8350109, 1628997… Back_Reef…                   0.68  6 Back Reef Slope ((1628948 8350136, 1628938… Back_Reef…                   0.45  7 Back Reef Slope ((1629017 8350158, 1629017… Back_Reef…                   0.52  8 Back Reef Slope ((1628929 8350162, 1628929… Back_Reef…                   0.76  9 Back Reef Slope ((1629009 8350189, 1629009… Back_Reef…                   0.64 10 Back Reef Slope ((1629014 8350189, 1629014… Back_Reef…                   0.47 # ℹ 659 more rows ggplot(lizard_seascape) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = settlement_probability), colour = NA) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(na.value = "grey90") +   labs(     fill = "Settlement\nprobability",     title = "Lizard Island settlement-probability seascape"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: End-to-end coralseed workflow: Lizard Island
text: seed_particles() takes particle positions external dispersal model adds biological filters, including time limits, competency, mortality, settlement probability. Inspect returned object.
code: lizard_particles <- seed_particles(   input = lizard_particles_sf,   zarr = FALSE,   set.centre = TRUE,   seascape = lizard_seascape,   probability = "additive",   limit.time = 12,   competency.function = "exponential",   crs = 20353,   simulate.mortality = "typeIII",   simulate.mortality.n = 0.1,   return.plot = TRUE,   return.summary = TRUE,   silent = FALSE ) names(lizard_particles) [1] "seed_particles" "multiplot"      "summary" lizard_particles$summary Metric               Value 1 Number of particle tracks                1000 2              Seed setting       [No seed set] 3                Start time          2022-12-16 4                  End time 2022-12-16 12:57:00 5      Dispersal time (hrs)               12.95 6   Total mortality by tmax                  70

title: End-to-end coralseed workflow: Lizard Island
text: settle_particles() applies spatial settlement probability returns simulated settled subset.
code: lizard_settlers <- settle_particles(   lizard_particles,   probability = "additive",   return.plot = FALSE,   silent = TRUE )  names(lizard_settlers) [1] "points" "paths"

title: End-to-end coralseed workflow: Lizard Island
code: plot_particles(   lizard_settlers$points,   lizard_seascape )

text: Interactive leaflet widgets can heavy pkgdown builds. stable documentation, live map_coralseed() call shown evaluated default. save stable HTML widget pkgdown: embed manually:

title: Seed particles, competency, and mortality
text: tutorial focuses seed_particles(): step converts particle-track output biologically filtered larval particles applying dispersal-time limits, competency, mortality, habitat settlement probability.
code: library(coralseed) library(tidyverse) library(sf) library(ggplot2)  sf::sf_use_s2(FALSE)

title: Seed particles, competency, and mortality
code: benthic_map <- system.file("extdata", "Lizard_Benthic.geojson", package = "coralseed") |>   st_read(quiet = TRUE)  reef_map <- system.file("extdata", "Lizard_Geomorphic.geojson", package = "coralseed") |>   st_read(quiet = TRUE)  seascape <- seascape_probability(   reefoutline = reef_map,   habitat = benthic_map )  particles_sf <- system.file("extdata", "lizard_del_14_1512_sim1_10.json", package = "coralseed") |>   st_read(quiet = TRUE)

title: Seed particles, competency, and mortality
code: seeded <- seed_particles(   input = particles_sf,   zarr = FALSE,   set.centre = TRUE,   seascape = seascape,   probability = "additive",   limit.time = 12,   competency.function = "exponential",   crs = 20353,   simulate.mortality = "typeIII",   simulate.mortality.n = 0.1,   return.plot = TRUE,   return.summary = TRUE,   silent = FALSE )

title: Seed particles, competency, and mortality
text: returned object includes points, inspect key fields:
code: names(seeded) [1] "seed_particles" "multiplot"      "summary" seeded$summary Metric               Value 1 Number of particle tracks                1000 2              Seed setting       [No seed set] 3                Start time          2022-12-16 4                  End time 2022-12-16 12:57:00 5      Dispersal time (hrs)               12.95 6   Total mortality by tmax                  79 seeded$points |>   st_drop_geometry() |>   glimpse() NULL

title: Seed particles, competency, and mortality
text: Use small scenario grid compare model behaviour.
code: competency_models <- c("exponential", "weibull", "lognormal")  seeded_competency <- purrr::map(   competency_models,   \(model) {     seed_particles(       input = particles_sf,       zarr = FALSE,       set.centre = TRUE,       seascape = seascape,       probability = "additive",       limit.time = 12,       competency.function = model,       crs = 20353,       simulate.mortality = "typeIII",       simulate.mortality.n = 0.1,       return.plot = FALSE,       return.summary = TRUE,       silent = TRUE     )   } ) |>   set_names(competency_models)

title: Seed particles, competency, and mortality
code: mortality_models <- c("typeI", "typeII", "typeIII")  seeded_mortality <- purrr::map(   mortality_models,   \(mortality) {     seed_particles(       input = particles_sf,       zarr = FALSE,       set.centre = TRUE,       seascape = seascape,       probability = "additive",       limit.time = 12,       competency.function = "exponential",       crs = 20353,       simulate.mortality = mortality,       simulate.mortality.n = 0.1,       return.plot = FALSE,       return.summary = TRUE,       silent = TRUE     )   } ) |>   set_names(mortality_models)

title: Seed particles, competency, and mortality
text: Use particles_to_tracks() related helpers need line features plotting spatial summaries.
code: tracks <- particles_to_tracks(seeded$points) tracks

title: Settlement, density, and spatial summaries
text: tutorial covers settlement end workflow: settle_particles(), settlement_density(), settlement_summary(), visual summaries settled larvae.
code: library(coralseed) library(tidyverse) library(sf) library(ggplot2)  sf::sf_use_s2(FALSE)

title: Settlement, density, and spatial summaries
code: benthic_map <- system.file("extdata", "Lizard_Benthic.geojson", package = "coralseed") |>   st_read(quiet = TRUE)  reef_map <- system.file("extdata", "Lizard_Geomorphic.geojson", package = "coralseed") |>   st_read(quiet = TRUE)  seascape <- seascape_probability(reefoutline = reef_map, habitat = benthic_map)  particles_sf <- system.file("extdata", "lizard_del_14_1512_sim1_10.json", package = "coralseed") |>   st_read(quiet = TRUE)  seeded <- seed_particles(   input = particles_sf,   zarr = FALSE,   set.centre = TRUE,   seascape = seascape,   probability = "additive",   limit.time = 12,   competency.function = "exponential",   crs = 20353,   simulate.mortality = "typeIII",   simulate.mortality.n = 0.1,   return.plot = FALSE,   return.summary = TRUE,   silent = TRUE )

title: Settlement, density, and spatial summaries
code: settled <- settle_particles(   seeded,   probability = "additive",   return.plot = FALSE,   silent = TRUE )  settled$points

what: Plot settled particles over the seascape
title: Settlement, density, and spatial summaries
code: plot_particles(settled$points, seascape)

title: Settlement, density, and spatial summaries
code: settlement_dens <- settlement_density(settled$points) settlement_dens ggplot() +   geom_sf(data = seascape, aes(fill = settlement_probability), colour = NA, alpha = 0.5) +   geom_sf(data = settlement_dens, aes(colour = n), size = 1) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(na.value = "grey90") +   scale_colour_viridis_c() +   labs(     fill = "Settlement\nprobability",     colour = "Settlers",     title = "Spatial density of settled particles"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Settlement, density, and spatial summaries
code: settlement_sum <- settlement_summary(   seeded,   settled,   cellsize = 50 )  settlement_sum

what: Summarise by distance from release centre
title: Settlement, density, and spatial summaries
code: release_centre <- seeded$centre  settlement_distance <- settled$points |>   st_transform(20353) |>   mutate(distance_m = as.numeric(st_distance(geometry, st_transform(release_centre, 20353)))) |>   st_drop_geometry() |>   mutate(distance_bin_m = cut(distance_m, breaks = seq(0, max(distance_m, na.rm = TRUE), by = 50))) |>   count(distance_bin_m)  settlement_distance

title: Compare larval reseeding scenarios
text: useful coralseed workflow compare alternative release biological scenarios. tutorial demonstrates repeatable pattern running several parameter combinations summarising expected settlement outcomes.
code: library(coralseed) library(tidyverse) library(sf) library(ggplot2)  sf::sf_use_s2(FALSE)

title: Compare larval reseeding scenarios
code: benthic_map <- system.file("extdata", "Lizard_Benthic.geojson", package = "coralseed") |>   st_read(quiet = TRUE)  reef_map <- system.file("extdata", "Lizard_Geomorphic.geojson", package = "coralseed") |>   st_read(quiet = TRUE)  seascape <- seascape_probability(reefoutline = reef_map, habitat = benthic_map)  particles_sf <- system.file("extdata", "lizard_del_14_1512_sim1_10.json", package = "coralseed") |>   st_read(quiet = TRUE)

title: Compare larval reseeding scenarios
code: scenarios <- tidyr::crossing(   scenario = c("low_mortality", "medium_mortality", "high_mortality"),   simulate_mortality_n = c(0.05, 0.10, 0.25) ) |>   distinct()  scenarios

title: Compare larval reseeding scenarios
code: run_scenario <- function(scenario, simulate_mortality_n) {   seeded <- seed_particles(     input = particles_sf,     zarr = FALSE,     set.centre = TRUE,     seascape = seascape,     probability = "additive",     limit.time = 12,     competency.function = "exponential",     crs = 20353,     simulate.mortality = "typeIII",     simulate.mortality.n = simulate_mortality_n,     return.plot = FALSE,     return.summary = TRUE,     silent = TRUE   )    settled <- settle_particles(     seeded,     probability = "additive",     return.plot = FALSE,     silent = TRUE   )    tibble(     scenario = scenario,     simulate_mortality_n = simulate_mortality_n,     n_particles = nrow(seeded$points),     n_settled = nrow(settled$points),     prop_settled = n_settled / n_particles   ) }

title: Compare larval reseeding scenarios
text: rendered documentation, can save scenario outputs data/ inst/extdata/ load .
code: scenario_results <- scenarios |>   pmap_dfr(run_scenario)  scenario_results

title: Compare larval reseeding scenarios
code: ggplot(scenario_results, aes(scenario, prop_settled)) +   geom_col() +   labs(     x = NULL,     y = "Proportion settled",     title = "Scenario comparison: expected settlement"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Compare larval reseeding scenarios
text: Potential scenario dimensions: release location; release timing; mortality type; competency function; release density multiplier; post-settlement survival; restoration plot size. Keep parameter sets table model run traceable reproducible.

title: Tiltmeter-derived flow fields and particle simulations
text: tutorial covers tiltmeter workflow added coralseed: aggregate tiltmeter velocities time; interpolate eastward northward velocities onto grid; simulate 2D stochastic Lagrangian particle trajectories gridded flow field; visualise flow fields particle tracks.
code: library(coralseed) library(tidyverse) library(sf) library(ggplot2) library(lubridate) library(terra)  sf::sf_use_s2(FALSE)

title: Tiltmeter-derived flow fields and particle simulations
text: tiltmeter input sf point object columns: Example preparation:
code: required_cols <- c(   "iso_8601_time",   "speed_cm_s",   "heading_degrees",   "velocity_n_cm_s",   "velocity_e_cm_s",   "inst",   "time",   "lat",   "lon" ) tiltmeters <- tiltmeters |>   mutate(     time = ymd_hms(iso_8601_time, tz = "UTC"),     inst = as.character(inst)   ) |>   st_as_sf(     coords = c("lon", "lat"),     crs = 4326,     remove = FALSE   )

title: Tiltmeter-derived flow fields and particle simulations
code: tiltmeters |>   st_drop_geometry() |>   mutate(inst = forcats::fct_reorder(inst, lat, .fun = mean, .desc = FALSE)) |>   ggplot(aes(time, speed_cm_s, group = inst)) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~ inst, nrow = 1) +   labs(     x = NULL,     y = "Speed (cm/s)",     title = "Tiltmeter speed time series ordered by latitude"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Tiltmeter-derived flow fields and particle simulations
text: simulate_flowfields() converts tiltmeter velocities cm/s m/s, aggregates time interval, builds spatial grid, interpolates u_ms v_ms separately.

title: 5: Particle dispersal simulation
text: simulate_flowfields() function takes point-based velocity observations tiltmeters spatially interpolates across study domain using inverse distance weighting (gstat::idw()) create time-varying two-dimensional flow field (user defined, 10m spatial resolution) timestep (user defined, 5 minute resolution). Interpolation restricted ocean domain, land polygons used define barriers exclude non-ocean areas. resulting gridded flow fields describe interpolated 2D current structure across logger array. flow fields derived spatial interpolation fixed-point measurements rather hydrodynamic model, represent observation-driven approximation near-bed logger-depth currents deployment period. Example tilt-meter derived flow-field 11:30:
code: flow_sim <- coralseed::simulate_flowfields(   tiltmeters = tiltmeters,   gbr_buffer = gbr_buffer,   crs_m = 32755,   grid_res_m = 10,   buffer_m = 250,   time_unit = "5 minutes",   idp = 2 ) library(terra)  grid_res <- 10 r_template <- terra::rast(   terra::ext(flow_sim$grid_sf),   resolution = grid_res,   crs = terra::crs(flow_sim$grid_sf) )  flow_raster <- flow_sim$flow_field |>   filter(time == ymd_hms("2025-11-17T11:30:00", tz = "UTC")) |>   mutate(     xend = x + u_ms * 10,     yend = y + v_ms * 10   ) |>    vect() |>   select(speed_ms) |>    rasterize(y=r_template, field = "speed_ms") |>   project(crs(land_poly)) #|>    #crop(land_poly, mask=FALSE)   flow_plot <- flow_sim$flow_field |>   filter(time == ymd_hms("2025-11-17T11:30:00", tz = "UTC")) |>   mutate(     xend = x + u_ms * 1200,     yend = y + v_ms * 1200   )    ggplot() +   tidyterra::geom_spatraster(     data = flow_raster,     alpha=0.8   ) +   geom_segment(     data = flow_plot |> st_drop_geometry(),     aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend),     arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.03, "inches")),     linewidth = 0.15,     alpha = 0.8,     colour = "black"   ) +   geom_sf(data = st_transform(land_poly, 32755), fill = "beige") +   coord_sf(crs = st_crs(32755)) +   theme_bw() +    xlab("Longitude") + ylab("Latitude") +   scale_fill_distiller(palette="RdBu", na.value = "transparent", name = "speed (m/s)")

title: 5: Particle dispersal simulation
text: simulate_particles() simulates passive particle dispersal using two-dimensional Lagrangian particle-tracking approach driven interpolated current-meter observations. Eastward northward velocity components tiltmeters averaged 60 s intervals spatially interpolated across bay generate time-varying flow field. Particles released user-defined fixed source locations (n=3) user-defined rate (n=1,000 particles) fixed rate (every 60 s 1 h, n=180,000 particles total). Particle positions updated 60 s timesteps using local interpolated velocities, horizontal diffusion represented random-walk term, *K** horizontal diffusion coefficient integrated random displacement per timestep (rnorm(n(), mean = 0, sd = sqrt(2 * K * dt))), random-walk standard deviation set sqrt(2 * 0.05 * 60) (.e. particle movemement per timestep = current displacement + random diffusion). Particles treated passive, depth-invariant tracers. avoid “beached” particles, particle intersects landits position reset previous “wet” location allowing re-advected next timestep retaining stochastic diffusion term. iterative re-release/random-walk procedure prevents particles becoming trapped land boundaries maintaining land impermeable barrier net transport. Importantly, model represents simplified, observation-driven 2D Lagrangian advection–diffusion simulation rather full hydrodynamic model. *K can set K = 0, particles follow interpolated currents diffusion, weak (K = 0.01), modest small-bay (K = 0.05), stronger (K = 0.1) spreading, strong coastal mixing (K = 1). Note - simulate_particles() uses furrr `future simulate independent particle groups parallel (180k individual simulated particles runs ~2.4mins) n=12 individual subsampled particle tracks particle dispersal model  Combined particle tracks subset 100 particle tracks full 12 hour period highlighting ) local retention within bay across 12hr period, ii) advection currents loss limited number particles western point, iii) loss particles eastern passage returning rocks change tidal currents ~8hrs post-release

simulate particles particle_sim <- coralseed::simulate_particles(   flow_field = flow_sim$flow_field,   land_poly = land_poly,   release_sites = release_sites,   crs_m = 32755,   dt = 5 * 60,   release_duration_seconds = 60 * 60,   n_particles_per_site_per_release = 100,   K = 0.05,   max_land_retry = 200,   seed = 101,   parallel = TRUE,   workers = 6 ) track_sample <- particle_sim$particle_tracks_sf |>   filter(particle_id %in% sample(unique(particle_id), 12))  ggplot() +   theme_bw() +   theme(     panel.background = element_rect(fill = "lightblue2", colour = NA),     #plot.background = element_rect(fill = "lightblue2", colour = NA),     panel.grid.major = element_blank(),     panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),     panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5),     plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0)   ) +   geom_sf(     data = land_poly,     fill = "forestgreen",     colour = NA   ) +    geom_sf(data=reef_poly, fill="wheat1", alpha=0.8) +   geom_path(     data = track_sample |> st_drop_geometry(),     aes(       x = x,       y = y,       group = particle_uid     ),     color="black",     alpha = 1,     linewidth = 1   ) +    geom_sf(     data = track_sample |> slice_max(n=1, order_by=time),     aes(group = particle_uid),     color="black", shape=21,     size = 2) + geom_sf(     data = track_sample |> slice_min(n=1, order_by=time),     aes(group = particle_uid),     color="black", shape=21, color="black",     size = 2) + geom_sf(     data = release_sites,     color="black", stroke=0.2,     size = 1, alpha=0.2   ) +  # title: coralseed [v1.1.0]: a spatially explicit probabilistic model of larval seeding for large scale restoration
text: Coastal ecosystems, including seagrasses, saltmarshes, mangroves, provide essential services shoreline stabilization, carbon sequestration, biodiversity support (Duarte et al., 2013). However, habitats face increasing degradation due anthropogenic pressures, climate change, habitat fragmentation (Waycott et al., 2009). result, restoration projects focusing propagule reseeding gained prominence scalable approaches restore ecosystem function biodiversity (Vanderklift et al. 2020). Fine-scale hydrodynamics, including tidal currents, eddies, vertical stratification, influence larval dispersal three-dimensional space (Cowen et al., 2000), larval swimming behaviour actively interacts water column structure makes dispersal predictions complex (Leis, 2007). complicating predictions larval dispersal understanding competency rates—time takes larvae capable settlement—influences dispersal window, affecting distance likelihood reaching suitable habitats (Graham et al. 2008, Connolly & Baird 2010). Even larvae competent, substrate electivity preferences specific settlement surfaces may prevent successful settlement (e.g., Harrington et al. 2004, Abdul Wahab et al 2023, Randall et al 2025), emphasizing need detailed habitat data, hydrodynamic models, larval behavioural parameterisation improve predictions. part due complexities, modelling tools effective finer spatial scales applied restoration operations (100s metres hectares; Bayraktarov et al. 2016) remain scarce, limiting ability predict broader spatial footprint outcomes larval reseeding efforts (see Gouezo et al. 2025 recent advancements). Access tools substantially improve resource managers capacity decide upon site selection optimise approaches facilitate evidence-based restoration plans assessments long-term ecological benefits. address challenges, developed ‘coralseed’, spatially explicit probabilistic model designed simulate larval dispersal settlement patterns following propagule releases. incorporating high-resolution oceanographic data, biological traits, habitat preferences, coralseed significantly improves spatial prediction restoration outcomes, facilitating dynamic decision making conservation science.

title: 2i. coralseed - Lizard Island (nGBR CONNIE)
code: library(coralseed) library(ggplot2) library(tidyverse) library(sf) library(tmap)    sf_use_s2(FALSE)    # load seascape   lizard_benthic_map <- system.file("extdata", "Lizard_Benthic.geojson", package = "coralseed") |>                               st_read(quiet=TRUE)  lizard_reef_map <- system.file("extdata", "Lizard_Geomorphic.geojson", package = "coralseed") |>                               st_read(quiet=TRUE)  lizard_seascape <- seascape_probability(reefoutline=lizard_reef_map, habitat=lizard_benthic_map)  # load particles lizard_particles_sf <- system.file("extdata", "lizard_del_14_1512_sim1_10.json", package = "coralseed") |> st_read(quiet=TRUE)   # run seed particles lizard_particles <- seed_particles(lizard_particles_sf,                               zarr = FALSE,                               set.centre = TRUE,                               seascape = lizard_seascape,                               probability = "additive",                               limit.time = 12,                               competency.function = "exponential",                               crs = 20353,                               simulate.mortality = "typeIII",                               simulate.mortality.n = 0.1,                               return.plot = TRUE,                               return.summary = TRUE,                               silent = FALSE)

title: 2i. coralseed - Lizard Island (nGBR CONNIE)
code: lizard_settlers <- settle_particles(lizard_particles,                                     probability = "additive",                                     return.plot=FALSE,                                     silent = TRUE)  plot_particles(lizard_settlers$points, lizard_seascape) lizard_settlement_density <- settlement_density(lizard_settlers$points) lizard_settlement_summary <- settlement_summary(lizard_particles, lizard_settlers, cellsize=50)

title: 2i. coralseed - Lizard Island (nGBR CONNIE)
code: map_coralseed(seed_particles_input = lizard_particles,               settle_particles_input = lizard_settlers,               settlement_density_input = lizard_settlement_density,               seascape_probability = lizard_seascape,               restoration.plot = c(100,100),               show.footprint = TRUE,               show.tracks = TRUE,               subsample = 1000,               webGL = TRUE)

title: 2i. coralseed - Lizard Island (nGBR CONNIE)
code: flowchart_coralseed(lizard_particles, lizard_settlers, multiplier=1000, postsettlement=0.8)

code: #library(coralseed) library(ggplot2) library(tidyverse) library(sf) library(tmap)    # load seascape   moore_benthic_map <- system.file("extdata", "Moore_Benthic.geojson", package = "coralseed") |>                               st_read(quiet=TRUE)  moore_reef_map <- system.file("extdata", "Moore_Geomorphic.geojson", package = "coralseed") |>                               st_read(quiet=TRUE)  moore_seascape <- seascape_probability(reefoutline=moore_reef_map, habitat=moore_benthic_map)  # load particles - import example zarr (oceanparcels output)   {zip_path <- system.file("extdata", "Moore_reseed.zip", package = "coralseed", mustWork = TRUE)   out_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "coralseed_Moore_reseed")   unlink(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, force = TRUE)   dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)   utils::unzip(zip_path, exdir = out_dir)}  moorereef_particles <- import_zarr(file.path(out_dir, "output.zarr"))   # run seed particles moore_particles <- seed_particles(moorereef_particles,                             set.centre = TRUE,                             seascape = moore_seascape,                             probability = "additive",                             limit_time = 7,                             competency.function = "exponential",                             crs = 20353,                             simulate.mortality = "typeII",                             simulate.mortality.n = 0.1,                             return.plot = TRUE,                             return.summary = TRUE,                             silent = FALSE)

title: coralseed Project
text: coralseed spatially explicit probabilistic model aims quantify spatial footprint coral larval re-seeding restoration progjects. model based input high-resolution oceanographic models (CONNIE) tracking individual particles space time. parameterising larval behaviour (competency, habitat specific substrate settlement preferences, swimming behaviour) individual-based probability settlement can modelled 105 - 107 larvae. Individual-based settlement probability dispersal paths overlaid onto high-resolution habitat maps (Allen Coral Atlas) form spatially-explicit model settlement following release coral larvae. coralseed aims provide insight key knowledge gaps larval restoration: larvae settle following release? spatial footprint larval reseeding projects? likely densities settled corals? densities adult corals (~10cm size) produced reseeding located? varying initial model parameters (e.g. tidal currents, time release, larval competency, larval densities, location release sites), simulation modelling approach can used quantify likely spatial footprint expected settlement densities, allowing optimisation upscaling larval reseeding programs Great Barrier Reef elsewhere.

title: coralseed Project
text: coralseed can installed github base parameterisation coralseed across 6.95hr time period (limit_time) exponential time--settlement model parameterisation (competency.function) type mortality curve (simulate.mortality) applied 10% population 24 hr period (simulate.mortality.n) additive settlement function (probability).
code: #install.packages("remotes") install_github("marine-ecologist/coralseed") library(coralseed) library(ggplot2) library(tidyverse) library(sf) library(tmap)  sf_use_s2(FALSE)  ## 0. Load example datasets  lizard_benthic_map <- st_read("../data-aux/Lizard-20250314042923/Benthic-Map/benthic.geojson", quiet=TRUE) lizard_reef_map <- st_read("../data-aux/Lizard-20250314042923/Geomorphic-Map/geomorphic.geojson", quiet=TRUE) lizard_particles <- st_read("../data-aux/run_day_12036_lizard_del_14_1512_sim1_10K_10.json", quiet=TRUE)    ## 1. simulate settlement probabilities from habitat maps # `seascape_probability` takes coral atlas inputs and a data.frame of settlement # probability (mean, se) to simulate probability of settlement in habitats surrounding the release area  lizard_seascape <- seascape_probability(reefoutline=lizard_reef_map, habitat=lizard_benthic_map)   ## 2. seed particles from dispersal model and simulate competency # `seed_particles` outputs summary statistics and a four panel diagnostic plot   lizard_particles <- seed_particles(input = "../data-aux/run_day_12036_lizard_del_14_1512_sim1_10K_10.json",                                    zarr = FALSE,                                    set.centre = TRUE,                                    seascape = lizard_seascape,                                    probability = "additive",                                    limit.time = 12,                                    competency.function = "exponential",                                    crs = 20353,                                    simulate.mortality = "typeIII",                                    simulate.mortality.n = 0.1,                                    return.plot = TRUE,                                    return.summary = TRUE,                                    silent = FALSE)                                     ## 3. simulate settlement of particles # `settle_particles` then applies probability across the seascape to simulate spatially # explicit patterns of settlement across the seascape:   lizard_settlers <- settle_particles(lizard_particles,                                     probability = "additive",                                     return.plot=FALSE,                                     silent = TRUE)  lizard_settlement_density <- settlement_density(lizard_settlers$points)   ## 4. Map combined coralseed outputs # `map_coralseed` visualises all outputs from the above three models    map_coralseed(seed_particles_input = lizard_particles,               settle_particles_input = lizard_settlers,               settlement_density_input = lizard_settlement_density,               seascape_probability = lizard_seascape,               restoration.plot = c(100,100),               show.footprint = TRUE,               show.tracks = TRUE,               subsample = 1000,               webGL = TRUE)

title: Simulate interpolated flow fields from tiltmeter velocity data — simulate_flowfields
text: Interpolates eastward northward velocity components tiltmeter observations onto regular spatial grid time step. function aggregates tiltmeter observations user-defined temporal resolution, converts velocities cm/s m/s, creates buffered spatial domain around instruments, uses inverse distance weighting generate gridded flow fields.

title: Simulate interpolated flow fields from tiltmeter velocity data — simulate_flowfields
code: simulate_flowfields(   tiltmeters,   gbr_buffer,   crs_m = 32755,   grid_res_m = 10,   buffer_m = 250,   time_unit = "5 minutes",   idp = 2 )

title: Simulate interpolated flow fields from tiltmeter velocity data — simulate_flowfields
text: tiltmeters sf object data frame containing tiltmeter observations. Must contain columns time, inst, lat, lon, velocity_e_cm_s, velocity_n_cm_s, speed_cm_s. gbr_buffer sf polygon object defining spatial mask reef buffer used constrain interpolation domain. crs_m Numeric character. Projected coordinate reference system used interpolation metres. Default 32755. grid_res_m Numeric. Grid cell spacing metres. Default 10. buffer_m Numeric. Buffer distance metres around convex hull tiltmeter locations. Default 250. time_unit Character. Temporal aggregation unit passed lubridate::floor_date(). Default "5 minutes". idp Numeric. Inverse distance weighting power passed gstat::idw(). Higher values give weight nearby observations. Default 2.

title: Simulate interpolated flow fields from tiltmeter velocity data — simulate_flowfields
text: named list : flow_field sf point object crs_m containing interpolated u_ms, v_ms, speed_ms, heading_degrees grid cell time step. flow_field_4326 interpolated flow field transformed EPSG:4326. tiltmeters_m Aggregated tiltmeter observations transformed crs_m. grid_sf interpolation grid sf point object crs_m. domain_m buffered interpolation domain clipped gbr_buffer.

title: Simulate interpolated flow fields from tiltmeter velocity data — simulate_flowfields
text: function first aggregates tiltmeter measurements instrument floored time interval. Eastward northward velocities converted cm/s m/s. convex hull constructed around tiltmeter locations, buffered buffer_m, clipped gbr_buffer. regular point grid generated domain. time step, inverse distance weighting applied separately eastward (u_ms) northward (v_ms) velocity components. Wind/current speed calculated : $$speed = \sqrt{u^2 + v^2}$$ Heading calculated degrees using: $$heading = atan2(u, v)$$ converted compass-style degrees 0 360. Time steps fewer three tiltmeter observations skipped.

what: Simulate Lagrangian particle trajectories from gridded flow fields — simulate_particles
title: Simulate Lagrangian particle trajectories from gridded flow fields — simulate_particles
text: Simulates passive particle dispersal one release sites using gridded eastward northward velocity fields. Particles released specified release window, advected using nearest available flow-field grid cell, moved added random-walk diffusion term. Candidate particle positions intersect land repeatedly redrawn maximum retry limit.

title: Simulate Lagrangian particle trajectories from gridded flow fields — simulate_particles
code: simulate_particles(   flow_field,   land_poly = NULL,   release_sites,   crs_m = 32755,   dt = 5 * 60,   release_duration_seconds = 60 * 60,   release_by = paste(dt, "sec"),   release_start = NULL,   n_particles_per_site_per_release = 100,   K = 0.05,   max_land_retry = 200,   seed = 101,   parallel = FALSE,   workers = NULL )

title: Simulate Lagrangian particle trajectories from gridded flow fields — simulate_particles
text: flow_field sf point object containing gridded flow fields. Must contain columns time, x, y, u_ms, v_ms. Velocities metres per second. land_poly sf polygon object representing land exclusion areas particles enter. Use NULL disable land exclusion. release_sites sf point object giving particle release locations. crs_m Numeric character. Projected coordinate reference system used particle movement metres. Default 32755. dt Numeric. Particle advection time step seconds. Default 5 * 60. release_duration_seconds Numeric. Duration particles released, seconds. Default 60 * 60. release_by Character. Interval release times, passed seq.POSIXt(). Default paste(dt, "sec"), release times align flow-field time steps. release_start Optional release start time. NULL, first available flow-field timestamp used. n_particles_per_site_per_release Integer. Number particles released per site release time. Default 100. K Numeric. Horizontal diffusivity used random-walk term, square metres per second. Default 0.05. max_land_retry Integer. Maximum number redraw attempts particles intersect land movement. Default 200. seed Integer. Random seed reproducible particle release diffusion. Default 101. parallel Logical. TRUE, run release sites parallel using future furrr. Default FALSE. workers Integer. Number parallel workers. NULL, uses one fewer number available cores.

title: Simulate Lagrangian particle trajectories from gridded flow fields — simulate_particles
text: named list : particle_tracks data frame containing particle positions time. particle_tracks_sf sf point object particle positions crs_m. particle_tracks_4326 sf point object particle positions transformed EPSG:4326. final_particles sf point object containing final recorded position particle crs_m. final_particles_4326 Final particle positions transformed EPSG:4326. particle_track_summary one-row summary table particle counts, track rows, land-retry diagnostics. particles0 Initial particle release table. release_sites_m Release sites transformed crs_m, release coordinates. land_m Land polygon transformed crs_m, NULL land_poly = NULL.

title: Simulate Lagrangian particle trajectories from gridded flow fields — simulate_particles
text: time loop sequential particle position depends previous position. Optional parallel processing therefore implemented splitting independent simulations across release sites. Particle movement follows: $$x_{t + 1} = x_t + u_t dt + \epsilon_x$$ $$y_{t + 1} = y_t + v_t dt + \epsilon_y$$ u_t v_t nearest-neighbour flow velocities \(\epsilon_x\) \(\epsilon_y\) normally distributed random-walk terms standard deviation: $$\sqrt{2 K dt}$$ Particles intersect land_poly movement redrawn previous position using advective diffusive step. particle still intersects land max_land_retry attempts, returned previous position time step. Flow fields matched exactly timestamp. reason, release_by = paste(dt, "sec") recommended unless flow field finer temporal resolution.
